leagueBriefing: Carlos Ortiz went nuclear with a career-best 10-under 60 to grab the early lead in Hong Kong, setting the tone on a softened Fanling track that saw scores pile up fast. Torque GC rode Ortiz's round into the team lead, while a packed chase group and a Victor Perez ace added to a wide-open Round 1 story.

games.headline: Ortiz fires career-best 60 to take two-shot lead as Torque tops teams in Hong Kong
games.brief: Carlos Ortiz flirted with something even sillier late, but a tap-in birdie on his final hole completed a 10-under 60 and a two-shot cushion after Round 1 at Hong Kong Golf Club. It was the lowest round of his pro tournament career and one of the rare 60s in LIV history, built on a blistering back nine that included a chip-in birdie and an eagle after sticking a par-5 approach to five feet. With the course softened by rain, scoring was there for the taking - and Torque GC took it, riding Ortiz's round into the team lead while Dean Burmester, Sergio Garcia and others stacked behind.
bullets: Carlos Ortiz shot 10-under 60 to lead by two shots over Dean Burmester (62).

Ortiz's 60 was the lowest round of his professional tournament career and the third 60 in LIV Golf history; only a 58 (Bryson DeChambeau) and two 59s (Joaquin Niemann, Sebastian Munoz) have been lower in league play.
Ortiz played his final nine holes at 8 under, highlighted by three straight birdies on holes 10-12 (including a chip-in at 11) and an eagle on the par-5 13th after hitting his second shot to 5 feet.
Torque GC led the team standings at 21 under (Ortiz 60, Munoz 65, Niemann 66, Ancer 68), two shots ahead of Smash GC at 19 under.
Victor Perez made the first ace of the LIV season by holing his tee shot at the 149-yard 2nd hole; it was the 13th ace in LIV Golf history.
games.context: With 28 players at 67 or better on a rain-softened Fanling course, Round 1 set up a weekend where rising wind is expected to toughen scoring - and the chase behind Ortiz is already crowded.

leagueBriefing: The BNP Paribas Open at Indian Wells opened with a chaotic first day filled with comeback wins and early upsets across both the ATP and WTA draws. Matteo Berrettini's emotional return victory highlighted the day, while several seeded players were pushed or eliminated as the desert tournament began taking shape.

games.headline: Berrettini comeback and early upsets headline opening day at Indian Wells
games.brief: Day 1 of the BNP Paribas Open produced the classic Indian Wells early-round chaos, where big names often face dangerous opponents immediately. Matteo Berrettini delivered the most dramatic moment of the day, battling through a three-set comeback victory to secure his first ATP Tour win in nearly a year before collapsing with cramps after match point. Elsewhere, Denis Shapovalov, Joao Fonseca and Gael Monfils moved safely into the second round, while the women's side saw several notable early exits as underdogs took advantage of the slow desert courts and unsettled seeds.
bullets: Matteo Berrettini won his first ATP Tour match in nearly 11 months with a comeback three-set victory.
Denis Shapovalov, Joao Fonseca and Gael Monfils advanced to the second round on the men's side.
Former Indian Wells champion Bianca Andreescu was upset by Kamilla Rakhimova in three sets.
Camila Osorio defeated Sloane Stephens 6-4, 6-1 in one of the day's most decisive WTA results.
Yulia Putintseva knocked out Paula Badosa 6-4, 6-2 in another notable women's upset.
games.context: Indian Wells is the first ATP Masters 1000 and one of the largest WTA events of the season, with top seeds entering in later rounds after first-round byes.
